title: Generic "From" for Email/Notification Automations
excerpt:
When creating an automation to send an email it would be useful to have a generic “From” instead of the user who created the automated email. 
For example, our organization has multiple use cases when a status changes to…

posts.author: bmauch (Ben Mauch)
posts.cookedHtml:
When creating an automation to send an email it would be useful to have a generic “From” instead of the user who created the automated email.
For example, our organization has multiple use cases when a status changes to something, then a team should receive a templated email.
If I am the one to create the email automation then all the emails that go out appear to be from me, which lead people to come back and ask why I am sending them an email on a topic. If it were to look like a generic automated email from a do-not-reply address they would more intuitively know it is really not a person sending an email out.

posts.author: eileen.rawlinson (Eileen Rawlinson)
posts.cookedHtml: We had this come up as well. There are teams who use mailboxes with a generic email address e.g. admin@organisation.com or accountspayable@organisation.com to send communications. That way whoever is working that day can see the replies and keep working on whatever the enquiry is. It would be great if you could specify the email address for any outgoing correspondence. It would also be good for instances where you don’t want the person to reply e.g. noreply@organisation.com.
